decks.notes: This deck focuses on fast mana acceleration with cards like Dark Ritual, Lotus Petal, and Lion's Eye Diamond to quickly assemble the pieces for a game-winning combo. The main combo involves using Burning Wish to tutor for The Fantasticar, which can be used to generate infinite mana with Mox Opal and Hexing Squelcher. This combo allows the deck to cast a massive Tendrils of Agony or Echo of Eons for a powerful finish. The deck also utilizes cards like Gaea's Will and Runehorn Hellkite to maximize its potential and disrupt opponents' strategies.

This deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's game plan while applying pressure with efficient creatures. By using cards like Daze, Force of Will, and Thoughtseize to counter and disrupt the opponent's plays, while also using efficient creatures like Barrowgoyf and Nethergoyf to close out the game quickly. The deck also includes some card advantage with cards like Mishra's Bauble, Ponder, and Brainstorm to maintain tempo and keep the pressure on the opponent. The deck aims to control the game early and finish off the opponent before they have a chance to recover.

The main concept of this deck is to disrupt and control the opponent while applying pressure with efficient creatures. By combining disruptive spells like Thoughtseize, Fatal Push, and Force of Will with powerful threats like Murktide Regent and Kaito, Bane of Nightmares, the deck aims to maintain tempo and stay ahead of the opponent. The inclusion of cards like Daze and Wasteland further emphasizes the importance of disrupting the opponent's game plan while also utilizing efficient card draw and selection through Ponder, Brainstorm, and Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student.

The deck revolves around assembling the combo involving Doomsday and Thassa's Oracle to win the game. It utilizes various ways to manipulate the top of the deck, such as Brainstorm and Ponder, to set up the combo efficiently. The Fantasticar is a crucial piece of the combo as it helps generate mana to cast Doomsday and protect the combo with counter spells like Force of Will. The deck aims to quickly assemble the combo and win the game before the opponent can disrupt it.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on aggressive strategies, utilizing creatures with efficient and powerful abilities such as Guide of Souls, Thalia, and Voice of Victory. The deck includes disruptive elements like Static Prison to control the opponent's board while also having access to removal spells like Swords to Plowshares. Ajani, Nacatl Pariah provides additional utility and can generate value through token generation. The deck aims to quickly overwhelm opponents with a combination of fast creatures, disruption, and powerful combat tricks to secure victory.

This deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's game plan while applying pressure with aggressive creatures. By utilizing cards like Stoneforge Mystic to search for powerful equipment and Thalia, Guardian of Thraben to tax the opponent's spells, the deck aims to control the battlefield and tempo of the game. The addition of Yorion, Sky Nomad as a companion allows for additional value and flexibility in longer games. Overall, the deck aims to maintain a disruptive game plan while applying pressure to overwhelm the opponent.

The main concept of this deck is to utilize artifacts like Chalice of the Void, Mox Opal, Mishra's Bauble, and Urza's Bauble to generate mana quickly. The deck aims to quickly assemble The Fantasticar, a powerful combo piece that can help generate additional mana and card advantage. Emry, Lurker of the Loch is used to recur artifacts from the graveyard, while Thought Monitor and Thoughtcast provide card draw to fuel the combo. Force of Will is included to protect the combo and disrupt opponent's strategies. The deck also includes lands like Ancient Tomb and Urza's Saga to further support the combo strategy.

This deck focuses on utilizing cheap and efficient spells to quickly control the board and pressure the opponent. It aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan with counterspells like Force of Will and Daze, while developing its own board presence with creatures like Dragon's Rage Channeler and Delver of Secrets. The deck also contains a strong land destruction package with Wasteland, further hindering the opponent's resources. By maintaining a quick and aggressive tempo, this deck aims to outpace and overwhelm the opponent before they can stabilize.

The deck focuses on controlling the opponent's board through a mix of counterspells, removal spells, and card draw. It aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan with cards like Force of Will and Swords to Plowshares, while also generating card advantage with Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student and Lórien Revealed. The deck also utilizes disruptive land cards like Wasteland to slow down the opponent's mana development. By maintaining control of the game and out-valuing the opponent, this deck aims to eventually win through a powerful finisher like Murktide Regent or True-Name Nemesis.

The main concept of this Boros Aggro deck is to pressure the opponent with efficient creatures like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Amped Raptor, while disrupting their game plan with cards like Thalia, Guardian of Thraben and Swords to Plowshares. The deck also utilizes Goblin Bombardment for additional reach and removal, as well as static effects like Static Prison to control the board. With a balance of aggression and disruption, this deck aims to overwhelm the opponent quickly and efficiently to secure victory in Legacy 1 vs 1 matches.

The key strategy of this deck is to use "Show and Tell" to put powerful creatures like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn or Atraxa, Grand Unifier onto the battlefield quickly, bypassing their mana cost and overwhelming the opponent. This is supported by card draw spells like Brainstorm and Ponder, as well as protection spells like Force of Will and Daze. By utilizing fast mana sources like Lotus Petal and Simian Spirit Guide, this deck aims to cheat out massive threats early in the game and win through overwhelming board presence.

The key concept of this deck is to assemble the combo of Painter's Servant and Grindstone to mill the opponent's entire library in one turn. The deck also utilizes artifacts like The Fantasticar, Manifold Key, and Voltaic Key to support the combo and generate mana quickly. Tezzeret, Karn, and Kozilek's Command provide versatility and control options, while cards like Urza's Saga and Urza's Tower provide additional mana acceleration. The deck aims to quickly set up its combo pieces and disrupt the opponent's game plan while establishing a dominant board presence.

The key strategy of this deck is to disrupt the opponent's game plan while maintaining board control through efficient removal spells and countermagic. By utilizing cards like Thoughtseize, Force of Will, and Wasteland, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's hand and mana base, while creatures like Orcish Bowmasters and Murktide Regent provide a clock to pressure the opponent. Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student and Kaito, Bane of Nightmares offer card advantage and versatility, allowing the deck to adapt to different matchups and strategies. Overall, this deck aims to win through a combination of disruption and aggression.

The main focus of this deck is to assemble the combo involving Doomsday and The Fantasticar to win the game. The deck includes key cards like Lotus Petal, Dark Ritual, and Lion's Eye Diamond to generate mana quickly, as well as cantrips like Brainstorm and Ponder to find the combo pieces. Interaction and protection are provided by cards like Force of Will and Thoughtseize. The deck aims to efficiently and effectively execute the combo while disrupting the opponent's strategy to secure victory in Legacy 1 vs 1 matches.

This deck focuses on quickly establishing board presence and disrupting the opponent's game plan. By utilizing aggressive creatures like Barrowgoyf, Rotting Regisaur, and Sheoldred, the Apocalypse, as well as powerful disruption pieces such as Chalice of the Void and Opposition Agent, the deck aims to control the game and overwhelm the opponent. The inclusion of mana acceleration cards like Chrome Mox and efficient removal like Snuff Out help in maintaining the tempo and efficiency of the deck. Additionally, cards like Trinisphere and The One Ring further disrupt the opponent's strategy and give the deck an advantage.

The main idea of this deck is to use The Fantasticar as a key combo piece to quickly assemble a win condition, such as Thassa's Oracle or Doomsday. The deck aims to be able to efficiently dig through its library using cards like Brainstorm and Ponder while also protecting its combo pieces with cards like Force of Will and Daze. The deck also includes various cantrips and ramp spells to help set up the combo and ensure a smooth and fast execution of the win condition.
